title: crypsinus majoensis
side_effects: Crypsinus majoensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also lead to skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering. In severe cases, improper use of Crypsinus majoensis can result in respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma-like symptoms, especially in individuals with pre-existing conditions. Additionally, improper preparation and consumption of the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers.
